text: I grow this for my cat in a small pot on my deck year ‘round (mild winters here) and she likes to chew on it daily. A pound of seeds lasts quite awhile for me.

text: I have always been a little fearful of fertilizing my peace lilies, tip burn especially.  Most generic houseplant foods, no matter how weak I made the solution always burned the tips.  I took a chance on this fertilizer, being especially formulated for peace lilies, thought this might work.  I ordered, read the directions and used just half of the recommended dosage on all of my peace lilies.  All of them, I have 6, turned deeply green in just two weeks and all threw out new larger florets.  I am so pleased with this unique formula.  I have been using it every three to four weeks, still at half strength.  At this rate, I will have fertilizer for a long time.  I highly recommend this peace lily fertilizer, at half strength of course.

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Fertilizante específico para lirios eliminó quemaduras de punta; plantas florecieron.

text: The first picture is of a perfect sundew as displayed on the 9 greenbox website.  Picture two is what it looked like after I pulled it from the box and the third picture is of it after I gave it a little wash in distilled water.  I would say this is right up there with the top 1 or 2 WORST looking plants that have ever been delivered. Sundews are supposed to be sticky.  This was so sticky that the soil stuck to it completely. Also WHY is this planted in soil?  The general method is to grow this like a venus flytrap with a mix of moist sphagnum peat most and some perlite.  It needs a rapid draining acidic soil.  A few people do add in a little soil but you can see what happens  when you do.  So basically I received 1/2 a plant with the other half being black - which in carnivorous terms means DEAD.  I would not have paid any amount for this plant - it's going to take extensive work to clean this up - and the soil (and rinsing) basically washed away all the sticky stuff that sundews use to catch insects.  I don't know if the remaining branches will regain their stickiness or if they'll need to die out and new ones will need to grow.  Based on  other reviewers - this plant MAY survive so I will cut off the black stuff and repot this in spaghum peat and perlite and keep it in a moist saucer of the water - watering from the saucer and NOT from the top (that's assuming there is a long healthy ball of roots that will run all the way to the end of the pot to pick up the water - otherwise I  may have to add a little soil or change my watering technique.  I should know not to buy from this seller but at the time there were no decent sundews being sold.  Big mistake.  Glad I didn't pay much.  Oh and if you are going to ship with soil TAPE under the soil to keep it intact - of course you'll  probably then lose buyers that will pull off the tendrils trying to unattach them from the soil - so instead,  just wrap it in damp very damp peat moss so it should arrive in better condition.  Wet peat moss should hopefully separate from the sundew sticky juice better than soil or tape.

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Planta recibida en condición deplorable comparada con la imagen del producto.

text: Absolutely gorgeous, healthy, vigorous large plants. They were packaged thanks nick! These plants are twice the size available at our local stores for the same price. They aren't picked over, leggy or even 1 hint of brown. These are truly amazing plants. Now it's hot where I live & they arrived perfect! They are shipped in individual pots, covered w/ a hard plastic formed shell. They were watered & shipped. Whether in individual packaged or 6 plants in 1 box they arrived in perfect condition. text:
Had bought this as canisters of loose powder in the past. Then the EPA comes in and makes it to where it can only be sold in the WSP water soluble packages. With the loose powder, I could make the product as strong as needed for my location (wayyyyyyyyyyy deep in the almost-forest woods, close to a major river, lots of creeks and leaves and trees and BUGGGGGGGGGSSSSSSSSS out the wazoo. No matter how clean your home, no matter how airtight your windows and doors, the wolf spiders and the house millipedes will find a way. Because the EPA mandates the way this is packaged and sold now, it makes the ratio needed for a once-a-year spray around my house ridiculously more expensive than it used to be...

The water soluble packs don't dissolve great. A couple of times I'd open the pump sprayer and there was still sticky pack with about half the contents of premeasured powder stuck to the wall of the sprayer. Already expensive and now kinda wasteful, too.

Still the best product around, IMO.

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Cambio de presentación por EPA afecta la dosis preferida; funcional pero con restricción.

text: There is no way to tell if it helped; however, after the 3rd treatment it resulted in leaf burn.

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Sin evidencia de efectividad y causó quemaduras en las hojas en el tercer tratamiento.

text: I wanted to try some in Alaska.  They have come back after 2 seasons but slowly.

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Plantas sobrevivieron dos temporadas en Alaska pero con crecimiento muy lento; resultado parcial.

text: Omg one night I put them on my basement and I caught so many cave crickets I'm so happy I found this💜

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Atrapó muchos grillos en el sótano en una sola noche; resultado entusiasta.

text: I have been using these devices for 20 years. Just turn it on wait 10 minutes and all the mosquitoes will be gone. Doesn’t kill them but repels them. I keep one in my vehicle one of my hunting bag. A couple around the house so I know I can always find one. You won’t find anything better to keep the mosquitoes away

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Repelente de mosquitos confiable con 20 años de uso; múltiples unidades para distintos lugares.

text: We have been using this inside of the house and around buildings in the yard. It works very good

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Funciona muy bien dentro y fuera de edificaciones para repeler insectos.

text: We have tons of fire ant hills here in Florida. I have found this to be the best product. You don’t really have to use as much as it says, I’ll put about a tablespoon or teaspoon depending on the size of the bed and the next day there are no more ants. I have found that after using it a lot the ants get used to it and will not eat it so  I have to change form with a different product for a few months and then go back to it.

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Mejor producto para hormigas de fuego en Florida; usa menos cantidad que lo recomendado.

text: Never revd

claude-sonnet-4-6.justificacion: Pedido nunca llegó; experiencia completamente negativa.

text: The seeds never sprouted. they died in the cold. They are not anymore hardy than any other tomato out there. I won't grow them again.
